---
title: "About"
title-block-style: none
pagetitle: "About | Mesfin Genie"
description: "Biography, expertise and professional background of Dr Mesfin Genie."
page-layout: full
image: /assets/img/og-card.png
---

```{=html}
<header class="page-header"><div class="page-shell narrow"><p class="eyebrow">About</p><h1>Health economics, behavioural economics and decision evidence</h1><p class="lead">Dr Mesfin Genie is a Continuing Senior Lecturer in Health Economics at Newcastle Business School, The University of Newcastle, Australia. His research examines how patients and the public value healthcare, process information and respond to policy. He works across health economics, behavioural economics, stated-preference methods, discrete choice experiments, eye tracking, health measurement and decision-support tools. His applied work spans organ transplantation, diabetes, prostate cancer, dementia, loneliness, telemedicine, medical devices, health workforce, vaccines, mandates and public-health interventions.</p></div></header>
<section class="content-section"><div class="page-shell narrow"><h2>Biography</h2><p>Dr Mesfin Genie is a Continuing Senior Lecturer in Health Economics at Newcastle Business School, The University of Newcastle, Australia. He received his PhD in Economics from Ca’ Foscari University of Venice, Italy, where he used stated-preference methods to study healthcare decision-making. He earned an MSc in Health Economics and Management from the University of Bologna in 2015, an MSc in Economics from Addis Ababa University, and a BA in Economics from Jimma University.</p><p>He has held research and teaching positions at Ca’ Foscari University of Venice, the Health Economics Research Unit at the University of Aberdeen, the Harrison School of Pharmacy at Auburn University, and the School of Medicine at Duke University.</p><h2>Education</h2><ul><li><strong>PhD in Economics</strong>, Ca’ Foscari University of Venice, Italy, 2019</li><li><strong>MSc in Health Economics and Management</strong>, University of Bologna, Italy, 2015</li><li><strong>MSc in Economics</strong>, Addis Ababa University, Ethiopia, 2010</li><li><strong>BA in Economics</strong>, Jimma University, Ethiopia, 2006</li></ul><h2>Research expertise</h2><p><span class="tag">Eye tracking</span><span class="tag">Behavioural economics</span><span class="tag">Clinical outcomes assessment</span><span class="tag">Discrete choice experiments</span><span class="tag">Health-preference research</span><span class="tag">Health economics</span><span class="tag">Stated-preference methods</span><span class="tag">Public-health policy</span><span class="tag">Decision-support tools</span></p><h2>Selected service</h2><p>Academic service includes serving as a Guest Editor for <em>Health Policy and Technology</em> and reviewing for journals including <em>Journal of Health Economics</em>, <em>Health Economics</em>, <em>Value in Health</em>, <em>Social Science & Medicine</em>, <em>Journal of Choice Modelling</em>, <em>Theory and Decision</em>, and <em>BMJ Open</em>.</p><h2>For prospective students</h2><p>I welcome enquiries from students interested in health economics, discrete choice experiments, eye-tracking methods, clinical outcomes assessment, behavioural economics and related areas. Prospective students should send a CV including academic qualifications, GPA or grading information, research experience and publications where applicable.</p></div></section>
```
